key_insight: SCAPS-1D plus machine learning efficiently ranked 125 perovskite solar cell stacks and identified lead-free double-perovskite architectures with very high predicted/measured efficiency.
engineering_relevance: The workflow provides a practical design map for choosing ETL, absorber, and HTL combinations to accelerate high-efficiency, environmentally friendlier perovskite solar cell development.
method: Trained an ML model on a representative subset to predict PCE for unseen stacks.
Used SHAP to identify the most influential descriptors and validate top candidates with full device simulations.
evidence_signal: Leave-One-Group-Out cross-validation showed strong rank-correlation performance, and the best verified devices reached 28.85% PCE (FTO/TiO2/Cs2AgBiBr6/NiO/Ag) and 28.62% PCE (FTO/SnO2/Cs2AgInBr6/NiO/Ag).
watch_item: The reported gains depend on simulation fidelity and the representativeness of the training subset, so experimental validation is still needed before deployment.
paper_title: Multilayer Screening of Double and Conventional Perovskite Solar Cells Using SCAPS-1D and Machine Learning: Optimization of ETL, HTL, and Absorber for High-Efficiency Architectures
